reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary: Reviewer Amir Goldstein noted that the patch is mostly good but suggested adding two files to the MAINTAINERS file, specifically the FILESYSTEMS [STACKABLE] section, and also mentioned a potential cleanup patch to remove unused functions d_backing_inode() and backing_inode_security{,_novalidate}(), which he offered to take care of if the submitter doesn't want to deal with it.
reviews.raw_body:
Nice!

Please add these files to
FILESYSTEMS [STACKABLE]
section in MAINTAINERS.

Otherwise, feel free to add:
Reviewed-by: Amir Goldstein <amir73il@gmail.com>

There is one more cleanup patch required, now or later, to get rid of
some awkwardness in kernel fs code.
If you don't want to deal with it, I can take this cleanup later.

d_backing_inode() is a noop from the day it was added and has 177
call sites. Those call sites should be converted to d_inode() and
d_backing_inode() should go away.

backing_inode_security{,_novalidate}() are likewise unneeded
dups of inode_security{,_novalidate}() and should be removed.

Thanks,
Amir.

2026-05-16.patch_summary: This patch adds a generic function, backing_inode_copyattr(), to the Linux kernel's fs/backing-inode.c file. The function copies inode attributes from a backing inode to a new inode, taking into account idmappings if present. This allows other filesystems that use backing inodes (such as fuse passthrough) to reuse this logic. The patch also moves the relevant code from overlayfs/util.c to the new generic function.
